title: erigeron ochroleucus var. hirtellus
intro: Erigeron ochroleucus var. hirtellus, also known as the hairy daisy or white fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The hairy daisy is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the hairy daisy is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
